patch.value: GH-258: strict unknown-key rejection across all structured CSV/markdown link & metadata parsers (validation parity with test --add)

pm files/docs --add (and sibling --add-glob/--remove/--migrate, plus create/update --dep/--dep-remove/--file/--doc and --reminder/--event/--type-option) silently DROP unknown CSV/markdown keys, while pm test --add strictly rejects them with an 'Allowed keys' error (pm-swie/B2 fix). This is a cross-command consistency defect: an agent that typos a key (lable= vs label=) gets silently wrong data. Fix: add a shared assertNoUnknownCsvKeys(kv, optionName, allowedKeys) helper mirroring the test --add message format and call it from every structured parser with an explicit allowed-key contract. Intentional plaintext-fallback parsers (--comment/--note/--learning, annotation --add) are deliberately excluded (unknown keys there mean 'treat as plaintext').

files/docs --add/--add-glob/--remove/--migrate and create/update --dep/--dep-remove/--file/--doc and --reminder/--event/--type-option reject unknown keys with the same 'does not recognize key X. Allowed keys: ...' error as test --add; plaintext-fallback parsers unchanged; 100% coverage; manual sandbox repro from GH-258 now errors.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implemented (GH-258). New shared core helper src/core/item/parse.ts:assertNoUnknownCsvKeys(kv, optionName, allowedKeys) — mirrors the test --add 'does not recognize key X. Allowed keys: ...' message format, case-insensitive allowed-key match (so a downstream-readable key like Path is never falsely rejected), USAGE exit code. Wired into every structured CSV/markdown parser with an explicit allowed-key contract: linked-artifacts.ts (--add path,scope,note / --add-glob pattern,glob,path,scope,note / --remove path / --migrate from,to), create.ts (--dep id,kind,type,author,created_at / --file,--doc path,scope,note), update.ts (--dep +source_kind / --dep-remove id,kind,type,source_kind), repeatable-metadata-parsers.ts (--reminder at,date,text,title / --event start,date,end,duration,title,description,location,timezone,all_day,recur_* / --type-option key,value). Bare (non-structured) values still skip validation. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: plaintext-fallback parsers (--comment/--note/--learning parseLogSeed, annotation --add parseAnnotationTextInput) — unknown keys there mean 'treat whole entry as plaintext', not an error; --field is schema-driven (open-ended custom field names). Tests added to parse-utils/files-docs/create-command/update-command specs; manual sandbox repro from the issue now errors with the exact allowed-key message. Docs/COMMANDS.md Linked Artifacts section updated.

patch.value: Shipped strict unknown-key rejection across structured CSV/markdown parsers: new shared assertNoUnknownCsvKeys helper (core/item/parse.ts) wired into files/docs --add/--add-glob/--remove/--migrate, create/update --dep/--dep-remove, create --file/--doc, and --reminder/--event/--type-option. Matches test --add 'Allowed keys' error format; plaintext-fallback parsers and schema-driven --field intentionally excluded. 100/100/100/100 coverage; docs updated.
